candidates.title: Enhancing interoceptive awareness in youth: Occupational therapy interventions for emotional regulation and mental health
Multiple Sclerosis as a Moderator in the Dynamics of Interoceptive Awareness and Emotion Dysregulation
A MULTICENTRIC OBSERVATIONAL AND EXPLORATORY STUDY ON REFLECTIVE FUNCTIONING AND INTEROCEPTIVE AWARENESS IN INDIVIDUALS WITH EATING DISORDERS.
candidates.tldr: The findings highlight the relevance of reflective functioning and interoceptive awareness as transdiagnostic dimensions in EDs and support the integration of therapeutic approaches targeting these mechanisms in the treatment of eating disorders.

candidates.title: The Interplay of Mindfulness, Interoception, and Dual Emotions in Enhancing Psychological Well-being – Development of Heal-thy Life Spiritual Psychology Assessment Scale (HLSPAS)
candidates.abstract: Recent developments in positive psychology and spiritual practices suggest a nuanced pathway to enhancing psychological well-being through the cultivation of mindfulness, interoception, and balanced dual emotions. This article explores the conceptual progression from mindfulness to interoception, further leading to an understanding of internal values and the strategic balancing of the dual emotions of silence and happiness. Mindfulness, defined as the non-judgmental focus on the present moment. Interoception, or the awareness of internal body sensations, complements mindfulness by enhancing self-regulation. The integration of silence and happiness as dual emotions contributes to mental clarity which is crucial for reducing symptoms of anxiety and depression. Happiness, associated with positive social interactions and compassion, plays a critical role in the promotion of relational well-being. By maintaining a balance between these emotions, individuals may experience enhanced well-being through improved hormonal balance and psychological resilience. Supported by spiritual practices that emphasize egolessness and contentment, this model proposes that mindfulness, interoception, and dual emotion management can synergistically foster a health-promoting environment, both psychologically and physiologically. This research involving development of Heal-thy Life Spiritual Psychology Assessment Scale (HLSPAS) aims to synthesize these elements into a coherent model that contributes to the broader understanding of psychological well-being, drawing from spiritual experience and practice in these areas.

candidates.title: A Comprehensive Model of Embodiment in Late Pubertal Female-at-Birth Adolescents: The Role of Body Awareness and Mental Health
candidates.abstract: Body awareness consists of aesthetic body image, functional body image, and interoception. Previous studies indicated a link between these components of body awareness and mental health. This study aims to clarify the relationship among these variables during the period of pubertal body changes. As puberty progresses, individuals’ perceptions of their bodies shift, which has been associated with a decline in mental health, according to the existing literature. To investigate this issue, a sample of 294 post-pubertal adolescents assigned female at birth completed assessments related to body awareness, mental health, psychosomatic symptoms, gender congruence, and eating disorders. A network analysis was conducted to illustrate the intricate interactions among the observed variables, and a mediation model was utilised to explore how body image influences overall health, with interoception and functional body image acting as mediators. The study identifies three key variables—body image, mental health, and interoception—as central within the network. Additionally, functional body image was significantly associated with other variables in the study. Ultimately, both direct and indirect effects of body image on mental health were found, mediated through interoception and functional body image. The clinical implications emphasise the importance of enhancing awareness of bodily sensations and functions to support psychological well-being, particularly during a developmental stage characterised by challenges related to body image due to rapid changes in puberty.

candidates.title: Interoception Accuracy Differences in Patients with Chronic Tension-type Headache
candidates.abstract:
ABSTRACT
 
 
 
 Tension-type headache (TTH) is the most common headache in the general population. Interoception refers to the perception of the body’s internal physiological state, in which bodily signals are detected, interpreted, and integrated by the nervous system. Interoception accuracy (IAc) is the ability to detect physiologic events occurring in the body. In this study, we aimed to evaluate the differences in IAc between patients with chronic TTH and healthy individuals.
 
 
 
 Fifty-one individuals with chronic TTH and fifty-one healthy volunteers were included in the study. Scales were administered to the participants. The heartbeat counting task was used to assess interoceptive accuracy (IAc).
 
 
 
 IAc was lower in the patient group than in the healthy group (t = −6.485; P < 0.001). The total score of the Beck Depression Scale was higher in the patient group than in the healthy group (Z = −4.706; P < 0.001). The total Beck Anxiety Scale score was also higher in the patient group than in the healthy group (t = 4.247; P = 0.001).
 
 
 
 Given the link between chronic pain and interoception, we propose that reduced IAc in chronic patients with TTH may be due to altered insular cortex function, a region integral to both pain processing and interoception.

candidates.title: Sexual Mindfulness and the Libido of Generativity: A Psychoanalytic Perspective on Future-Oriented Desire and Couple Well-Being
candidates.abstract: This perspective advances a psychoanalytic—embodiment account of the “libido of generativity” (LoG)—future-oriented reorganization of erotic desire that links embodied arousal with caregiving, legacy, and shared projects. We define LoG along four axes (direction of investment: dyad↔triad; temporal horizon: immediacy↔future; outcome modalities: procreative, creative–sublimative, community-forming; affective regulation: shame/guilt↔pride/gratitude). Integrating interoception, body ownership/agency, and self-compassion with reproductive mentalizing, we specify three proximal levers—embodiment, affect regulation, and representation—through which sexual mindfulness can recalibrate bodily salience, blunt shame-based self-objectification, and expand triadic representations (self–partner–child/symbolic offspring). We then translate these mechanisms into a brief, practice-ready relationship guidance (RG) curriculum (6–8 sessions) combining somatic mindfulness, compassion micro-practices, reproductive-mentalizing dialogs, communication skills, and generative rituals. We articulate falsifiable propositions (e.g., mindfulness → ↑couple satisfaction via ↓body-image self-consciousness and ↑reproductive mentalizing) and a sex/gender-attentive reporting plan (SAGER). Primary outcomes include sexual functioning/satisfaction and couple satisfaction/communication; secondary mechanistic endpoints index interoceptive accuracy/awareness (including heartbeat-evoked potentials), self-compassion, and reproductive mentalizing. By aligning contemplative practices with couple-skills training and equity-focused implementation, the LoG framework offers a testable bridge from psychoanalytic theory and embodiment science to measurable improvements in sexual health and couple well-being.

candidates.title: Investigating Associations between Maternal Mindfulness, Mental Health, Interoception and Mother-Infant Relationship during Pregnancy and Post-Partum in a Non-Clinical Community Sample
candidates.abstract: Pregnancy and the postpartum period are times of significant transition for women, with changes in maternal physical and mental health. The relationship between a mother and her infant has been recognised as laying the foundation for later child development. There is considerable evidence that this early relationship is influenced by a woman’s wellbeing during pregnancy and in the early post-partum period. Mindfulness has been found to have positive health outcomes, primarily in reducing maternal stress, anxiety, and depression. However, there remains a need for considering the influence of mindfulness on the mother-infant relationship during pregnancy and the first trimester post-partum. Given the limited research on interoception in pregnancy, this maternal variable was included to acknowledge the importance of embodiment in maternal mindfulness, mental health, and mother-infant relationship. Its relevance was assessed through the associations with all the other measures. This study was the first to explore associations between maternal mindfulness, mental health (depression, anxiety, and stress), interoception, and mother-infant relationship during pregnancy and post-partum across three time points in a non-clinical community sample. This can provide possible avenues for assessment and support strategies by pre and perinatal healthcare practitioners. Women were assessed at 20+ weeks gestation (110), approximately 36-week gestation (72) and 10-12 weeks postpartum (67). Their age ranged from 18 to 47 (M = 33.22). The majority were from Australia and the rest from New Zealand, UK, USA, and Canada. They were from middle to upper class socioeconomic background and from high school/equivalent to doctoral degree educational level, with the majority holding a bachelor’s degree. The maternal variables were assessed with self-report questionnaires. Changes in the maternal variables during pregnancy and postpartum across three timepoints were analysed using ANOVA. Correlati

candidates.title: The Impact of Subjective Interoceptive Accuracy on Opinion Change: How Exposure to Opposing Views Influences Perceptions of Events
candidates.venue: International Journal of Social Sciences and Public Administration
candidates.abstract: This research examines the connection between subjective interoceptive accuracy and the tendency to maintain or alter opinions when individuals were confronted with opposing views. We proposed that individuals exhibiting higher interoceptive accuracy would be less inclined to change their opinions. Surprisingly, our findings did not reveal a significant association between interoceptive accuracy and the extent of opinion change. Notably, however, we identified a moderate association between an increased sensitivity to internal bodily signals-as indexed by the Noticing subscale of the Multidimensional Assessment of Interoceptive Awareness (MAIA-N)-and a higher likelihood of opinion change.  This observation implies that individuals who are more attuned to their internal bodily signals might also be more adaptable in revising their perspectives when exposed to contradictory viewpoints. Overall, these results enhance our understanding of interoceptive accuracy and attitude shifting, highlighting the intricate interplay between bodily sensations and higher-order cognitive processes.

candidates.title: Movement as Preventive Education for Females at Risk of Anorexia Nervosa: Effects of Physical-Cognitive Interventions on Psychophysiological Markers
candidates.venue: Journal of Kinesiology and Exercise Sciences
candidates.abstract: Background: Anorexia nervosa (AN) is a complex psychiatric disorder posing major challenges to treatment. Effective early prevention requires interdisciplinary approaches integrating physical activity with health education. The aim of this study was to evaluate the preventive potential of structured body-oriented interventions by comparing their effectiveness in improving subjective and physiological well-being among young women at risk of developing AN. Methods: Forty-two women exhibiting Anorexia Readiness Syndrome (ARS) symptoms were randomly assigned to three Nalu. Mindful Movement interventions: Dance Movement Therapy (DMT), yoga, and mindfulness. Well-being was assessed subjectively (stress, anxiety, mood, eating disorder symptoms) and objectively (heart rate and heart rate variability parameters). Measurements were taken at baseline, after a four-week control period, and following a four-week intervention. Interoceptive awareness was examined as a potential mediator of psychophysiological resilience to AN risk. Results: Mixed-design repeated-measures ANOVA showed no changes in psychometric measures and a stress-like physiological decline during the control period. All interventions improved subjective well-being, while physiological improvement occurred after yoga and mindfulness training. Yoga produced the strongest overall effects. Regression analyses indicated that ARS scores were predicted by body dissatisfaction and low self-esteem, both linked to interoceptive deficits and trait anxiety. Improvements in self-esteem and body dissatisfaction were associated with intervention-related increases in interoceptive awareness. Conclusion: Integrative physical–mental practices may serve as effective preventive strategies against eating disorders by enhancing interoceptive awareness and emotional regulation. These findings support the inclusion of such interventions within health education and exercise science curricula targeting at-risk populations.

candidates.title: Gut Feelings, Interoception, and Emotional Resilience: Revealing the Connection
candidates.abstract: Interoception – the sensory system responsible for interpreting internal bodily signals – is progressively emerging as a central concept in mental health research. This paper investigates how interoception influences the gut-brain axis, emotional regulation, and resilience, highlighting the bidirectional relationship between gut microbiota and brain processes. Animal research has convincingly demonstrated that gut microbes impact behaviour and anxiety; however, human research is restricted by theoretical and methodological challenges. While therapeutic approaches targeted at internal bodily awareness and gut health show promising results, further research is needed to examine their scalability and applicability in diverse cultural contexts. This paper also raises concerns regarding the risk of over-medicalising normal bodily sensations and advocates for a more integrated mind-body approach within clinical settings. This work contributes a unique perspective by synthesising interoceptive research with philosophical considerations of mind-body dualism, while critically analysing how cultural contexts shape embodied emotional experience. By embracing the value of interoception, psychological frameworks can evolve into more grounded and proactive practices, cultivating emotional awareness and psychological wellbeing.

candidates.title: The Association between Interoception and Mental Health
